title: The Deepest Loom-Thread

features.name: spool descending
features.desc-on-touch: Thread coiled impossibly deep, vanishing into black loam. Your fingers follow it down. The thread never tangles or ends.

features.name: woven earth
features.desc-on-touch: Loom patterns continue here without the loom. Threads have woven themselves into the soil. The weaving descends.

features.name: knot-memory
features.desc-on-touch: A burl in the thread itself, readable as text. Each knot holds a pattern. Each pattern is a memory the weaving caught.

features.name: hands still tracing
features.desc-on-touch: Marks in the soil where fingertips have traced the thread-patterns. Recent marks. The hands do not know what knowledge they are reading.

features.name: thread archive
features.desc-on-touch: Specimen cards pinned to bark. Thread samples labeled by depth. Each is a century older than the one above. The dates run backward from today.

inscription: the loom weaves in darkness; the darkness learns the loom's language

file.name: Loom_Tactile_Survey_Depth_Recordings.mp3
file.note: Voices describing thread-texture by touch alone. Each speaker adds to the description. The recordings layer across decades, comparing touches.

exits.label: where the thread splits and tangles
deeper still, toward the foundation
exits.to: NEW: the ancient spool where weaving began
